standup.name: Smit Parekh
inProgress: LibreOffice headless letter conversion on stage — code done, blocked on env (net.dll JVM shutdown crash + stale-build deploys); need monarch-portal runtime log on port 8084
Stage deploy diagnosis with DevOps — identifying correct service log and verifying LibreOffice warm-up
Relieving letter blank-body letterhead-merge fix — not yet verified end-to-end
next: Confirm LibreOffice warm-up OK in monarch-portal log, then deploy to prod
Build HR/admin edit of exit dates (confirmed/submitted/proposed LWD) once block-when-COMPLETED + settlement-recompute decisions are made

tasks.name: LibreOffice headless letter conversion reliability
tasks.body: Hardened the DOCX->PDF (LibreOffice/Word) path so it runs under the Windows LocalSystem service account.
tasks.summary:
- Added headless flags (--norestore, --nofirststartwizard, --nolockcheck) and per-run isolated profile
- Fixed the broken timeout (process output to file so waitFor actually governs) — was blocking forever on a hang
- Resolve soffice by file existence (not a flaky --version probe that fails under Session 0)
- Cache the path at startup + background warm-up to avoid slow cold start
- Made LibreOffice path + DevOps-provisioned C:/LibreOfficeProfile configurable

tasks.name: Match letter PDF to MD DOCX template
tasks.body: Made the generated letters reproduce the MD-provided letterhead and exact wording.
tasks.summary:
- Extracted logo + Great Place To Work footer badge from letterhead.docx, embedded as base64
- Built fixed header (logo top-right) and footer (company address/CIN/GSTIN/DUNS + contact + badge)
- Reproduced exact relieving & experience letter wording from the MD templates
- Letterhead shown in production only (stage = plain)

tasks.name: Dual letter engine + two generate buttons
tasks.body: Let HR generate letters via either the Word .docx template or pure-Java HTML.
tasks.summary:
- Added LetterEngine enum; generate(exitId, engine) on relieving/experience services
- ?engine=TEMPLATE (LibreOffice) or HTML (openhtmltopdf, default) on the controller
- Frontend: DocumentCard second button; wired Word Template + Quick PDF actions
- Updated RTK Query mutations to pass the engine

tasks.name: Stage deploy diagnosis (net.dll JVM crash)
tasks.body: Diagnosed why stage letter generation still failed after deploy.
tasks.summary:
- Identified a stale build was deployed (engine param ignored -> openhtmltopdf in logs)
- Confirmed all code merged into dev (PR #258, d1f2a12); triggered rebuild via Jenkins pipeline
- Identified user-supplied logs were the wrong service (legacy MiplNewBackendApplication) and a JVM hs_err crash
- Diagnosed the net.dll crash as a Windows NetworkInterface.getAll() shutdown issue (JVM/driver), unrelated to letter code; suggested -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true

tasks.name: Embed Arial font + fix footer entity crash
tasks.body: Fixed loose letter spacing and a latent production crash in HTML letters.
tasks.summary:
- Registered real Arial TTF with openhtmltopdf (was falling back to base-14 Helvetica with approximate widths)
- Verified via rendered PDF: embeds ArialMT/Arial-BoldMT subsets, crisp spacing
- Caught & fixed   in footer (strict XHTML rejects it -> would crash letterhead generation)
- Replaced non-breaking hyphens (rendered as #) with plain hyphens; fixed footer column widths so email no longer wraps

tasks.name: Fix relieving letter blank body (letterhead merge detection)
tasks.body: Continued the letterhead-merge body-detection fix in DocxTemplateService.
tasks.summary:
- isFooterAddressElement scoped to CIN/GSTIN/DUNS + Dwarkesh markers
- Folded into the broader TEMPLATE-engine pipeline now deployed
- End-to-end body verification still pending on a working stage LibreOffice run
